commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the `talk.config(includeSecrets)` early return, returns runtime-resolved Talk provider config in `talk.resolved.config` for scoped callers, and adds handler-level SecretRef tests.

PR surface: Source -3, Tests +81. Total +78 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-backed: current main returns early for `includeSecrets:true`, and the iOS parser only accepts a string `apiKey`. I did not run a live native iOS session in this read-only review.

target.title: fix(gateway): iOS Talk treats SecretRef-backed API keys as missing
target.body:
Closes #98209
Related: #91020

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where iOS native Talk users could see `Voice API key missing` for ElevenLabs when the Gateway had already resolved `talk.providers.elevenlabs.apiKey` from a SecretRef. The failure happens because scoped `talk.config({ includeSecrets: true })` returned the source-shaped SecretRef object before the runtime-resolved Talk config path ran, while iOS only treats `talk.resolved.config.apiKey` as configured when it is a string.

## Why This Change Was Made

The Gateway now lets the existing runtime-resolved Talk response path run for callers that already have `operator.talk.secrets`, and uses that resolved provider config only for `talk.resolved.config`. Ordinary read-scope callers still get the source-shaped value restored before redaction, so runtime secret values are not exposed outside the Talk secret scope.

This intentionally keeps `talk.providers.<id>` source-shaped. A prior attempt (#91020) replaced the same early-return seam but raised the native credential-boundary question; this version keeps the fix narrow and relies on the documented `operator.talk.secrets` native-read scope without changing the provider-map contract.

## User Impact

Users who pair the iOS app with Talk secret scope can use SecretRef-backed ElevenLabs native Talk configuration without copying the provider key into plaintext config or entering a separate local mobile key. Users without Talk secret scope continue to see redacted config and do not receive the resolved runtime key.

## Evidence

Issue proof:

- #98209 records the real Windows/iOS incident: OpenClaw `2026.6.11-beta.2`, iOS paired with Talk secret scope, ElevenLabs native Talk showed `Voice API key missing` while the same key worked when temporarily stored as plaintext.
- Source-level before proof on current `main` (`6cb82eaab865`): `resolveTalkResponseFromConfig` returned early for `includeSecrets`, so the runtime-resolved SecretRef path was skipped.
- iOS source proof: `TalkModeGatewayConfigParser` reads `activeConfig["apiKey"]?.stringValue`, so a SecretRef object in `talk.resolved.config.apiKey` is treated as missing.

After-fix local proof:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts --testNamePattern="talk.config"
```

Result: `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 6 passed | 60 skipped (66)`.

```text
pnpm exec oxlint src/gateway/server-methods/talk.ts src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Result: both exited cleanly.

```text
$env:PYTHONUTF8='1'; python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local
```

Result: `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

What was not tested: no physical iOS app session against this branch and no live ElevenLabs synthesis request. The test uses fake credential strings only; no real API key is included in the issue, PR body, tests, or commit.
